source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Suppose the input voltages $V_{1}$, $V_{2}$, and $V_{3}$ in the circuit of Fig. 3.1 can assume values of either 0 or 1 (0 means ground). There are thus 8 possible combinations of input voltage. Compute $V_{\text{out}}$ for each of these possibilities.

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Suppose the input voltages $V_{1}$, $V_{2}$, and $V_{3}$ in the circuit of Fig. 3.1 can assume values of either 0 or 1 (0 means ground). There are thus 8 possible combinations of input voltage. Compute $V_{\text{out}}$ for each of these possibilities.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL object string representation rather than a file path or URL
answer provides a general formula for V_out rather than listing all 8 input combinations explicitly
metadata.reasoning_chain: The circuit in Fig. 3.1 can be redrawn as that in Fig. 3.2. Let the currents flowing in the component circuits be as shown. By Kirchhoff's laws we have $V_{3} =\left[2\left(i_{3}-i_{2}\right)+2\left(i_{3}-i_{4}\right)\right] R$, $V_{2}-V_{3} =\left[2\left(i_{2}-i_{1}\right)+(i_{2}-i_{4})+2\left(i_{2}-i_{3}\right)\right] R$, $V_{1}-V_{2} =\left[2 i_{1}+(i_{1}-i_{4})+2\left(i_{1}-i_{2}\right)\right] R$, $0 =2\left(i_{4}-i_{3}\right)+(i_{4}-i_{2})+(i_{4}-i_{1})+2 i_{4}$. After solving for $i_{4}$ we obtain $V_{\text{out}}=2 i_{4} R=\frac{V_{1}}{3}+\frac{V_{2}}{6}+\frac{V_{3}}{12}$.

By Kirchhoff's laws we have $V_{3} =\left[2\left(i_{3}-i_{2}\right)+2\left(i_{3}-i_{4}\right)\right] R$, $V_{2}-V_{3} =\left[2\left(i_{2}-i_{1}\right)+(i_{2}-i_{4})+2\left(i_{2}-i_{3}\right)\right] R$, $V_{1}-V_{2} =\left[2 i_{1}+(i_{1}-i_{4})+2\left(i_{1}-i_{2}\right)\right] R$, $0 =2\left(i_{4}-i_{3}\right)+(i_{4}-i_{2})+(i_{4}-i_{1})+2 i_{4}$. After solving for $i_{4}$ we obtain $V_{\text{out}}=2 i_{4} R=\frac{V_{1}}{3}+\frac{V_{2}}{6}+\frac{V_{3}}{12}$.
